title: Bicorona philliraeoides
taxonomy: Bicorona philliraeoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the bittersweet family. This family consists of around 900 species, including trees, shrubs, and climbing plants. The Celastraceae family is part of the larger order Celastrales, which also includes other plant families such as Parnassiaceae and Hippocastanaceae. Bicorona philliraeoides is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, specifically in the Solomon Islands, and is classified as an endemic species. The exact classification of Bicorona philliraeoides within the plant kingdom is still a subject of ongoing research and classification refinement.
